summary.intent: Resize the model dropdown to fit its content ('Off/Low/Medium/High') instead of using a hardcoded fixed width.
summary.outcome: The fix required overriding the width in layout.css (not just the Tailwind/component CSS), which was amended into the previous commit.

repo: The app loads both individual CSS files (e.g. layout.css) AND a compiled style.css in dev mode — changes to only one may not be reflected in the browser.
layout.css contains component-level overrides like .model-dropdown { width: 280px } that take precedence and must be updated independently of other stylesheets.

code.finding: .model-dropdown has a hardcoded width: 280px that overrides any Tailwind or component-level CSS changes.

workflow: When a CSS change has no visible effect, check for duplicate style definitions across multiple CSS files loaded in dev mode.
CSS cascade specificity can be misleading — even if individual files load before style.css, a more specific selector in layout.css will win.

friction: Initial fix was pushed without catching that layout.css also defined the same property, causing no visible change in the browser.
Dev mode loads styles from multiple sources (individual files + compiled bundle), making it non-obvious which file is authoritative.
